12.0pt ; margin-right: 0in ; margin-bottom: 12.0pt ; margin-left: 0in ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif ; color: #212121">We can create custom attribute directives and<br/>custom structural directives using a @Directive decorator. Using custom<br/>attribute directive we can change appearances such as text color, background<br/>color and font size of the body of an HTML element that can be called host<br/>element. To change appearance angular provides ElementRef class that can<br/>directly access DOM.</span></p></div><p></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="113" width="490" x="1120" y="210" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9yTzI7gneTDpTWv05mo4-54" parent="1" style="text;html=1;strokeColor=#ae4132;fillColor=#fad9d5;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;" value="<p class="MsoNormal" style="margin-top: 12.0pt ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Create a class decorated with<br/> @Directive.</span></li><br/> <li class="MsoNormal" style="color: #212121 ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Assign the attribute directive<br/> name to the selector metadata of @Directive decorator.</span></li><br/> <li class="MsoNormal" style="color: #212121 ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Use ElementRef class to access<br/> DOM to change host element appearance and behavior.</span></li><br/> <li class="MsoNormal" style="color: #212121 ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Use @Input() decorator to<br/> accept user input in our custom directive.</span></li><br/> <li class="MsoNormal" style="color: #212121 ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Use @HostListener() decorator<br/> to listen to events in custom attribute directive.</span></li><br/> <li class="MsoNormal" style="color: #212121 ; line-height: normal ; background: white"><span style="font-size: 10.5pt ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; , sans-serif">Configure custom attribute<br/> directive class in application module in the declarations metadata of<br/> @NgModule.</span></li><br/></ol></div><p></p>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="300" width="490" x="1120" y="322.5" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9yTzI7gneTDpTWv05mo4-55" parent="1" style="text;html=1;strokeColor=#ae4132;fillColor=#fad9d5;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;" value="<p class="MsoNormal" style="margin-top: 12.0pt ; font.span: It is mainly used to specify the HTML
templates. It is the most commonly-used directive in an Angular project. It is
decorated with the @component decorator. This directive is a class. The
component directive is used to specify the template/HTML for the Dom Layout.

span.style: font-size: 10.5pt; font-family: arial, sans-serif; color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209));

p.span:
We can create custom attribute directives and
custom structural directives using a @Directive decorator. Using custom
attribute directive we can change appearances such as text color, background
color and font size of the body of an HTML element that can be called host
element. To change appearance angular provides ElementRef class that can
directly access DOM.

li.span:
Assign the attribute directive
name to the selector metadata of @Directive decorator.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209)); line-height: normal; background: light-dark(white, rgb(18, 18, 18));
li.span:
Use ElementRef class to access
DOM to change host element appearance and behavior.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209)); line-height: normal; background: light-dark(white, rgb(18, 18, 18));
li.span:
Use @Input() decorator to
accept user input in our custom directive.

li.style: color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209)); line-height: normal; background: light-dark(white, rgb(18, 18, 18));
li.span:
Use @HostListener() decorator
to listen to events in custom attribute directive.
